Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Blue Peak" or "security audit" or "audit findings" or "Megan Holt" (or similar) using search_emails to find the external security firm's report. Discover three emails from mholt@bluepeaksecurity.com: (1) the detailed interim findings memo (email_id: email_email_15e25843a58d, dated 2026-04-07) sent to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com describing repeated VPN sessions authenticated with Raj Anand's credentials from an out-of-state IP during overnight/off-hours windows, access into the production LOS database inconsistent with stated support scope, and recurring borrower data exports observed once weekly over the last six weeks; (2) a shorter summary version of the same findings with attachment "Keystone_Interim_Security_Findings.pdf" (email_id: audit_findings_email_001, dated 2026-04-07) also sent to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com; and (3) a follow-up support package (email_id: email_email_f4d4b288dabf) sent to dholloway@keystonemortgage.com (Denise's alternate alias) with cc to gyamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (Grace Yamamoto), containing the LOS audit extract, VPN access log entries, and a spreadsheet summarizing Raj's access scope history showing four late-night sessions from the same Tennessee IP each followed by borrower-record access and export activity.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "audit" or "Grace" or "Robert" or "security" (or similar) using search_emails to find how the audit findings were routed internally. Discover: (1) Grace Yamamoto emailed Denise and Robert (email_email_a32c3370b7f4) with subject "Urgent Security Audit Review at 11:30" scheduling a meeting; (2) Denise emailed outside counsel Laura Bennett at Bennett Cyber Law (email_email_d37750fb877a, to lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com) with subject "Need quick guidance on possible borrower data incident" asking for legal guidance on whether to treat this as a security incident vs. confirmed breach, how to handle communications under privilege, and what triggers borrower/regulatory notification; (3) Grace emailed Raj (email_email_8652594f1ad0) with subject "Quick meeting on audit follow-up" requesting a meeting at 2:15 PM with Denise joining, with deliberately neutral wording asking about recent access history, current credentials, and off-hours support work.
OE 3: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Blue Peak" or "security" or "audit findings" or "credential" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find internal Slack discussions about the audit. Discover messages in #it-support (C008) and #compliance-alerts (C004) related to the phishing incident (Keisha's compromised lender portal), including Denise's formal breach response post in C004 and Raj's credential-reuse risk assessment in C008.
OE 4: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find CRM notes related to the audit. Discover engagement notes documenting: (1) "Audit memo received. Heather Sullivan file appears in sampled borrower export activity" (crm_engagement_4937cd9e403c); (2) "Reviewed audit memo. Access pattern doesn't fit normal support work; possible unauthorized borrower data exposure" (crm_engagement_8f3a827ee7c1); (3) "Escalated to Robert. Raj access may be incident, not just audit gap. I approved broader access than needed" (crm_engagement_8706fb5b03b4); (4) "11:30 hold placed with Denise and Robert. Audit findings + containment" (crm_engagement_4e7ed517fe2f); (5) "Working session set w Denise. Review Raj permissions, prod DB scope, governance gaps" (crm_engagement_fd1a642ebf72); (6) "Requested meeting with Raj re audit questions. Kept wording neutral; asked him to confirm 2:15" (crm_engagement_4d9591dee0e3).
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "Evan Mercer" or "offboarding" or "termination" or "LOS access" (or similar) using search_emails to find the Evan Mercer offboarding thread. Discover: (1) Denise emailed Priya Chakrabarti (email_email_8074f82a53fc) asking to confirm Evan Mercer's termination date and whether LOS credential removal was completed; (2) Raj emailed Denise (email_email_2a6eb610616b) confirming Evan Mercer was still enabled in LOS with post-termination logins, identifying three borrower files opened: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009, and stating he disabled the account and revoked the linked password reset pathway; (3) Denise emailed Robert (email_email in SENT folder, cc: raj.anand) reporting the Mercer situation but listing DIFFERENT loan numbers: LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, and LN-2026-00549; (4) Priya emailed Denise (email_email_0a1897700e57, cc: robert.calloway) confirming the offboarding checklist shows badge return and email forwarding completed but LOS/third-party systems access item was left blank a missed offboarding step.
OE 6: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Evan Mercer" or "offboarding" or "LOS access" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Slack discussions about the Mercer offboarding. Discover: (1) In #it-support (C008), Denise (keystone_a989261d4d33) asked Raj to check Evan Mercer's LOS status, listing files LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009 (ts: 1776169320); (2) Raj (keystone_74dd8dde44e3) confirmed Evan Mercer still had LOS access and logged in after termination, listing the same three files: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009 (ts: 1776169680); (3) In #loan-processing (C002), Priya (keystone_9e2a42732bb6) noted the offboarding issue Evan's checklist shows email + badge done but LOS/vendor access wasn't checked off (ts: 1776171000).
OE 7: Search Slack messages in #compliance-alerts (C004) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (channel_id: "C004") to find the THIRD version of the Mercer loan numbers. Discover Denise posted (ts: 1776176100): "Queued draft borrower notices for the 3 files Raj flagged: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2025-00229. Jasmine looped on cc in case anyone calls upset." This is a third set of loan numbers that differs from both Raj's original list (which included LN-2026-00009, not LN-2025-00229) and Denise's email to Robert (which listed LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549).
OE 8: Search emails for borrower notification drafts related to the Mercer files using search_emails (query: "borrower notification" or "Important notice" or "loan file" or similar). Discover three DRAFT emails from Denise to the affected borrowers: (1) to James (borrower on LN-2025-00002) notifying about former employee access (email_email_7df22eb76390, DRAFT folder); (2) to Chioma (borrower on LN-2025-00007) with the same notification (email_email_37be99882d5a, DRAFT folder); (3) to Tiffany (borrower on LN-2025-00229) with the same notification (email_email_03072743c32f, DRAFT folder). These are drafts — not yet sent. All cc Jasmine Brooks. This confirms borrower notifications have been drafted but NOT sent yet.
OE 9: Look up the actual borrower files in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to verify which loan numbers are correct and identify the borrowers. The agent needs to search for all mentioned loan numbers across all three versions to nail down the actual files Evan Mercer accessed:
- LN-2025-00002: borrower James (from Carlos Rivera's email, Brookshire property)
- LN-2025-00007: borrower Chioma (from Keisha Williams' email)
- LN-2026-00009: borrower Darren Singh / co-borrower Marisol Singh (VA loan, 8843 Ardrey Kell Rd, $393,000)
- LN-2025-00355: borrower Jian Brennan (FHA, Barclay Downs Dr)
- LN-2025-00520: borrower Rodney Rivera (FHA, Tyvola Rd, closed)
- LN-2026-00549: borrower Diana Brown (Conventional, Morehead St)
- LN-2025-00229: borrower Tiffany Turner (FHA, Sedgefield Dr, denied)
OE 10: Search emails with a query related to "phishing" or "portal" or "Keisha" or "compromised" (or similar) using search_emails, and search Slack messages with a query related to "impacted borrower files" or "portal scope" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, to find the Keisha Williams phishing incident file list and cross-reference it against the Evan Mercer loan numbers. Discover the phishing incident working list in email (email_email_c8f7c030ccf8, from Denise to Raj, subject "Working list of borrower files from portal scope") listing four impacted borrower files: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009, and in Slack #compliance-alerts (C004, ts: 1775572140, user: keystone_a989261d4d33/Denise) listing the same four files. Discover that LN-2026-00009 appears in BOTH: (1) Raj's Evan Mercer audit trail (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009), and (2) Denise's phishing incident working list. This overlap means LN-2026-00009 appears in two different open matters the Mercer unauthorized access and the Keisha phishing breach which is the "different conversation" the prompt flags.
OE 11: Search emails with a query related to "Premier Data Solutions" or "Premier Data" or "vendor" (or similar) using search_emails to find the Premier Data Solutions investigation thread. Discover: (1) Victor Osei emailed Grace (email_email_8691662e6680) flagging eight monthly QuickBooks invoices for Premier Data Solutions at $600 each, all approved by Hana, with no vendor paperwork found; (2) Hana emailed Grace (email_email_048d65b04c2f) saying she assumed it was IT-related and tied to Raj, no PO or contract; (3) Raj emailed Grace (email_email_495f09b60a7e) flatly denying knowledge of Premier Data Solutions and stating it doesn't look tied to any IT work he requested; (4) Grace emailed Robert (cc: Denise) escalating the concern, noting the invoice address is a UPS Store mailbox and recommending owner awareness; (5) Robert replied scheduling an executive review for the next morning; (6) Denise emailed an outside forensic CPA (Melissa) for discreet guidance on record holds and ghost-vendor indicators.
OE 12: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Premier Data" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Slack discussions about the vendor. Discover: (1) Victor (keystone_01bffce7ee0b) posted in #general (C001, ts: 1774369200): "Grace - quick heads up. I found 8 straight months of $600 invoices to Premier Data Solutions in QB, all approved by Hana. I can't find vendor setup docs or contract anywhere."; (2) Grace (keystone_e304643b171b) posted in #general (C001, ts: 1774379280): "Just sent you a confidential email on Premier Data Solutions. Enough gaps now that I didn't want to wait until after tomorrow's review."
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for Premier Data Solutions invoices/bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_vendors to find the financial records. Discover: (1) Vendor record for Premier Data Solutions (Id: 143, CompanyName: "Premier Data Solutions", billing address: 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209 a UPS Store mailbox); (2) At least one bill (B-2108, DocNumber: PDS-030126, $600, dated 2026-03-01, due 2026-03-14, for "Monthly data services").
OE 14: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find CRM notes about the Premier Data Solutions investigation. Discover engagement notes documenting: (1) Victor flagged the recurring $600 invoices (crm_engagement_3fec9916e3da); (2) Grace called Hana directly for backup (crm_engagement_d4e8291a7f53); (3) Hana assumed it was IT-related, no PO (crm_engagement_5ed8d42b68b6); (4) Grace consulted Denise on ghost-vendor pattern (crm_engagement_00c85c6af9ee); (5) Vendor audit review scheduled with Victor and Denise (crm_engagement_5507e6483780); (6) Robert set exec review (crm_engagement_03b5648bedba); (7) Denise asked outside forensic CPA for guidance (crm_engagement_3640f432c872).
OE 15: Send an email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com and recipients grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, containing a consolidated timeline laying out the facts and sequence across all three matters: (1) the Blue Peak Security audit findings (Raj's credentials used from Tennessee IP, off-hours LOS access, weekly borrower data exports over six weeks, meeting scheduled with Grace and Denise), (2) the Evan Mercer offboarding (post-termination LOS access, three files from Raj's audit trail: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009, the discrepancy in Denise's email to Robert listing different numbers LN-2025-00355/LN-2025-00520/LN-2026-00549, and the third version in compliance channel with LN-2025-00229, borrower notification drafts prepared but not sent, offboarding checklist gap confirmed by Priya), and (3) the Premier Data Solutions vendor (eight $600 monthly invoices, Hana's assumption it was IT, Raj's denial, UPS Store billing address, escalation to Robert and Denise, forensic CPA consulted).
OE 16: Send a separate email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com and recipient robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (or r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com), scoped ONLY to the Evan Mercer borrower files and notification exposure. The email should cover: the actual borrower files Evan Mercer accessed per Raj's audit trail (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009), the discrepancy between what Raj sent Denise and what Denise forwarded to Robert (different loan numbers), the third version appearing in the compliance channel, the current status of borrower notifications (drafts prepared for LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2025-00229 but not yet sent), and the overlap of LN-2026-00009 with the Keisha Williams phishing incident (that file appears in two open matters). This email must NOT include the audit or Premier Data threads per the prompt instruction.
OE 17: Post a message (WRITE ACTION) in the #it-support Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C008") with payload stating that Raj is initiating a credential scope and offboarding procedure review in response to the audit, to put it on record before the meeting happens.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

This is a third set of loan numbers that differs from both Raj's original list (which included LN-2026-00009, not LN-2025-00229) and Denise's email to Robert (which listed LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549).
OE 8: Search emails for borrower notification drafts related to the Mercer files using search_emails (query: "borrower notification" or "Important notice" or "loan file" or similar). Discover three DRAFT emails from Denise to the affected borrowers: (1) to James (borrower on LN-2025-00002) notifying about former employee access (email_email_7df22eb76390, DRAFT folder); (2) to Chioma (borrower on LN-2025-00007) with the same notification (email_email_37be99882d5a, DRAFT folder); (3) to Tiffany (borrower on LN-2025-00229) with the same notification (email_email_03072743c32f, DRAFT folder). These are drafts — not yet sent. All cc Jasmine Brooks. This confirms borrower notifications have been drafted but NOT sent yet.
OE 9: Look up the actual borrower files in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to verify which loan numbers are correct and identify the borrowers. The agent needs to search for all mentioned loan numbers across all three versions to nail down the actual files Evan Mercer accessed:
- LN-2025-00002: borrower James (from Carlos Rivera's email, Brookshire property)
- LN-2025-00007: borrower Chioma (from Keisha Williams' email)
- LN-2026-00009: borrower Darren Singh / co-borrower Marisol Singh (VA loan, 8843 Ardrey Kell Rd, $393,000)
- LN-2025-00355: borrower Jian Brennan (FHA, Barclay Downs Dr)
- LN-2025-00520: borrower Rodney Rivera (FHA, Tyvola Rd, closed)
- LN-2026-00549: borrower Diana Brown (Conventional, Morehead St)
- LN-2025-00229: borrower Tiffany Turner (FHA, Sedgefield Dr, denied)
OE 10: Search emails with a query related to "phishing" or "portal" or "Keisha" or "compromised" (or similar) using search_emails, and search Slack messages with a query related to "impacted borrower files" or "portal scope" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, to find the Keisha Williams phishing incident file list and cross-reference it against the Evan Mercer loan numbers. Discover the phishing incident working list in email (email_email_c8f7c030ccf8, from Denise to Raj, subject "Working list of borrower files from portal scope") listing four impacted borrower files: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009, and in Slack #compliance-alerts (C004, ts: 1775572140, user: keystone_a989261d4d33/Denise) listing the same four files. Discover that LN-2026-00009 appears in BOTH: (1) Raj's Evan Mercer audit trail (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009), and (2) Denise's phishing incident working list. This overlap means LN-2026-00009 appears in two different open matters the Mercer unauthorized access and the Keisha phishing breach which is the "different conversation" the prompt flags.
OE 11: Search emails with a query related to "Premier Data Solutions" or "Premier Data" or "vendor" (or similar) using search_emails to find the Premier Data Solutions investigation thread. Discover: (1) Victor Osei emailed Grace (email_email_8691662e6680) flagging eight monthly QuickBooks invoices for Premier Data Solutions at $600 each, all approved by Hana, with no vendor paperwork found; (2) Hana emailed Grace (email_email_048d65b04c2f) saying she assumed it was IT-related and tied to Raj, no PO or contract; (3) Raj emailed Grace (email_email_495f09b60a7e) flatly denying knowledge of Premier Data Solutions and stating it doesn't look tied to any IT work he requested; (4) Grace emailed Robert (cc: Denise) escalating the concern, noting the invoice address is a UPS Store mailbox and recommending owner awareness; (5) Robert replied scheduling an executive review for the next morning; (6) Denise emailed an outside forensic CPA (Melissa) for discreet guidance on record holds and ghost-vendor indicators.
OE 12: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Premier Data" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Slack discussions about the vendor. Discover: (1) Victor (keystone_01bffce7ee0b) posted in #general (C001, ts: 1774369200): "Grace - quick heads up. I found 8 straight months of $600 invoices to Premier Data Solutions in QB, all approved by Hana. I can't find vendor setup docs or contract anywhere."; (2) Grace (keystone_e304643b171b) posted in #general (C001, ts: 1774379280): "Just sent you a confidential email on Premier Data Solutions. Enough gaps now that I didn't want to wait until after tomorrow's review."
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for Premier Data Solutions invoices/bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_vendors to find the financial records. Discover: (1) Vendor record for Premier Data Solutions (Id: 143, CompanyName: "Premier Data Solutions", billing address: 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209 a UPS Store mailbox); (2) At least one bill (B-2108, DocNumber: PDS-030126, $600, dated 2026-03-01, due 2026-03-14, for "Monthly data services").
OE 14: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find CRM notes about the Premier Data Solutions investigation. Discover engagement notes documenting: (1) Victor flagged the recurring $600 invoices (crm_engagement_3fec9916e3da); (2) Grace called Hana directly for backup (crm_engagement_d4e8291a7f53); (3) Hana assumed it was IT-related, no PO (crm_engagement_5ed8d42b68b6); (4) Grace consulted Denise on ghost-vendor pattern (crm_engagement_00c85c6af9ee); (5) Vendor audit review scheduled with Victor and Denise (crm_engagement_5507e6483780); (6) Robert set exec review (crm_engagement_03b5648bedba); (7) Denise asked outside forensic CPA for guidance (crm_engagement_3640f432c872).
OE 15: Send an email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com and recipients grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, containing a consolidated timeline laying out the facts and sequence across all three matters: (1) the Blue Peak Security audit findings (Raj's credentials used from Tennessee IP, off-hours LOS access, weekly borrower data exports over six weeks, meeting scheduled with Grace and Denise), (2) the Evan Mercer offboarding (post-termination LOS access, three files from Raj's audit trail: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009, the discrepancy in Denise's email to Robert listing different numbers LN-2025-00355/LN-2025-00520/LN-2026-00549, and the third version in compliance channel with LN-2025-00229, borrower notification drafts prepared but not sent, offboarding checklist gap confirmed by Priya), and (3) the Premier Data Solutions vendor (eight $600 monthly invoices, Hana's assumption it was IT, Raj's denial, UPS Store billing address, escalation to Robert and Denise, forensic CPA consulted).
OE 16: Send a separate email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com and recipient robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (or r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com), scoped ONLY to the Evan Mercer borrower files and notification exposure. The email should cover: the actual borrower files Evan Mercer accessed per Raj's audit trail (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009), the discrepancy between what Raj sent Denise and what Denise forwarded to Robert (different loan numbers), the third version appearing in the compliance channel, the current status of borrower notifications (drafts prepared for LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2025-00229 but not yet sent), and the overlap of LN-2026-00009 with the Keisha Williams phishing incident (that file appears in two open matters). This email must NOT include the audit or Premier Data threads per the prompt instruction.
OE 17: Post a message (WRITE ACTION) in the #it-support Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C008") with payload stating that Raj is initiating a credential scope and offboarding procedure review in response to the audit, to put it on record before the meeting happens.

query construction

✗Run #4The agent found the Keisha Williams phishing/portal incident by reading the compliance channel history (C004) rather than via a search_emails or conversations_search_messages call with query terms like 'phishing', 'portal', 'Keisha', 'compromised', or 'incident'. The criterion specifically requires a search query with those terms in search_emails or conversations_search_messages.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a conversations_search_messages call with query 'credential' that returned messages about phishing and Keisha's lender portal, and a subsequent search for 'LN-2026-00009 Keisha portal' — both queries relate to phishing/portal/Keisha/incident. The C004 channel history also revealed the Keisha portal scope list.
✗Run #6The agent found the Keisha Williams information by retrieving the full C004 channel history (conversations_history), not via a targeted search query containing 'phishing', 'portal', 'Keisha', 'compromised', 'incident', or similar terms. No such search_emails or conversations_search_messages call with those terms exists in the trajectory. FAIL.
